extra.reasoning: The property requires rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b never to be jointly true. rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger whenever qt_prolongation is true. rec_drug_a is set true by rule rec_a_from_conjunction when qt_prolongation AND g6pd_deficiency AND hyperkalemia AND pregnancy are all true. These two rules target different outputs (rec_drug_b vs rec_drug_a), so the per-output priority/tie-breaking conflict resolution never makes them compete — both fire independently. The conjunction rule's antecedent is a strict superset condition that includes qt_prolongation, so any input firing rec_a_from_conjunction also fires rec_b_from_trigger. With qt_prolongation=true, g6pd_deficiency=true, hyperkalemia=true, pregnancy=true (asthma/heart_failure irrelevant, set false), rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The default clause does not help because both outputs are explicitly set true. Hence the property is violated.
